source_pr.title: planner: wrong slice length check for index merge's order prop push down
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/43178

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Wrong slice-bound check, so the out-of-bound panic raised.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: panic: out-of-bound slice access in index-merge order property pushdown (planner/core/task.go)
failure_signature: panic: index out of range in `PhysicalTopN.checkOrderPropForSubIndexScan` (planner/core/task.go)
root_cause_explanation: A planner bug could cause an out-of-bound panic while pushing down order properties for IndexMerge plans. Specifically, `checkOrderPropForSubIndexScan` used an incorrect slice-bound check and could index `constColsByCond[idxPos]` when `idxPos == len(constColsByCond)`, triggering `panic: index out of range` during planning/explain for certain queries. This PR fixes the boundary condition (`idxPos >= len(...)`) and adds a regression issue test to ensure the `EXPLAIN ... use_index_merge ... ORDER BY ... LIMIT ...` case does not panic.
fix_pattern: Fix slice bounds checks in planner property propagation (e.g., use `idxPos >= len(slice)` before indexing) and add a regression test that executes the problematic `EXPLAIN` query to ensure no panic.

evidence: evidence: + race = "on", | tk.MustExec("explain select /*+ use_index_merge( `aa311c3c` ) */ `aa311c3c`.`43b06e99` as r0 , `aa311c3c`.`6302d8ac` as r1 from `aa311c3c` where IsNull( `aa311c3c`.`b80b3746` ) or not( `aa311c3c`.`57fd8d09` >= '2008' ) order by r0,r1 limit 95") (planner/core/casetest/physical_plan_test.go)

Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
